| Metric | Previous | Current | Change |

**Note:** This is the first Rust-era coverage report. The TypeScript codebase (with JSDoc) had near-100% coverage. The Rust codebase uses `///` doc comments; some adapter and port trait methods lack them.

**No regressions detected** — this is a baseline for the new Rust codebase.

## Scope Notes

- **In scope:** All `pub` items (functions, structs, enums, traits, methods, constants) across 6 crates
- **Out of scope:** `pub(crate)` items, test modules, `main()` entry point
- **Documentation definition:** A symbol is "documented" if it has a `///` doc comment immediately preceding its declaration
